name: Anomaly-mediated SUSY breaking (AMSB)
summary: Mediates SUSY breaking from a hidden sector to the visible sector through the super-Weyl (conformal) anomaly. SUSY-breaking sector is sequestered on a separate brane, and gaugino / scalar masses arise at one loop from the conformal anomaly. Produces a distinctive spectrum dominated by wino LSP and degenerate sleptons, with a wide gravitino-LSP mass gap.

addresses_puzzles.note: Implementation choice within MSSM: requires SUSY to address the hierarchy problem. Provides a specific mechanism for soft-SUSY-breaking parameters.
Wino-LSP DM in AMSB has well-defined mass (~ 2.7-3 TeV for thermal abundance) and annihilation cross-section; Fermi-LAT dwarf and HESS galactic-center constraints exclude or constrain wide windows.

touches_benchmarks.note: Sommerfeld-enhanced wino annihilation to W+ W- gives sharp gamma-ray signals; Fermi-LAT and HESS bounds disfavour thermal wino DM at ~3 TeV in much of the parameter space.
Slepton-chargino loops contribute to (g-2)_mu at levels comparable to MSSM more generally; AMSB's degenerate slepton spectrum gives somewhat suppressed contributions.

introduces.note: Builds on MSSM; new parameters are the gravitino mass m_3/2 (~ 30-100 TeV) and tan(beta), with scalar/gaugino masses determined by anomalous dimensions.

history: Randall and Sundrum (1998), 'Out of this world supersymmetry breaking', Nucl. Phys. B 557, 79; Giudice, Luty, Murayama, Rattazzi (1998), 'Gaugino mass without singlets', JHEP 12, 027. Active phenomenology through 2000s-2010s; pure-AMSB tachyonic-slepton problem requires UV modifications.

references.citation: Randall and Sundrum, 'Out of this world supersymmetry breaking', Nucl. Phys. B 557, 79 (1999)
Giudice, Luty, Murayama, Rattazzi, 'Gaugino mass without singlets', JHEP 12, 027 (1998)
